rationale: The citing paper repeatedly uses Van Heerde, Gijsbrechts, and Pauwels (2008) as a key prior study on the Dutch supermarket price war, not just as a passing reference. It relies on that paper's findings (e.g., increased consumer price sensitivity during a price war) to set up its conceptual framework and to motivate baseline direct effects that its own model augments. The cited work is also used to ground the empirical context and interpretation of chain-level outcomes in the same price-war episode. This indicates a meaningful build-on relationship rather than a generic literature-list mention.
relation_description: The 2015 study extends Van Heerde et al. (2008) by moving from direct price-war outcomes to a broader system that adds media coverage as an endogenous driver of retailer, consumer, and investor responses. It treats the earlier paper's documented price-war effects as a starting point and develops additional media-mediated mechanisms on top of that baseline.
